reviews.title: It’s all about making money
reviews.text:
The resorts number one priority is making money. Service is last on the agenda. 

Do not expect a level of service that excels others.. goodwill is not part of their vocabulary. 

Do yourselves a favour and spend this money on a partner resort like the Ritz where customer service is the number one priority.

reviews.title: 5 star chain hotel from hell
reviews.text:
During our stay we have a series of mistreatment from the Hotel, several catastrophical error was that we must have a mandatory 150 dollar new years eve dinner per person, which we oblige, however after being ping pong back and forth between front office and the ball recipient team (due to wrong tickets being issued to us) we found out at the event that our family table didn't exist. so they ask us to stand up in the middle of the ballroom with embarrassment. 

another incident near the swimming pool, I went to a water fountain to refresh with a cup of water. However upon walking there I step on a bottle shards with sharp edges that caused blood splash all over the place. I yelled at the staff there to ask help but one of the staff was just looking at me and continue to give towels to the nearby guess without any empathy. after attending my wounds all by myself, finally the hotel doctor came. it was a deep open wound, hence several stitches needed. no apologies offered by the manager
reviews.room_type: Family Suite, 2 Bedroom Suite, Bedroom 1: 1 King, Bedroom 2: 2 Double

reviews.title: If you're a family with young ones, I do not recommend this hotel.
reviews.positives: Breakfast was great. That's all.
reviews.negatives:
Everything other than breakfast. We have 2 little kids, the room layout was awful for a family. We stayed at another resort before this which was excellent. we booked the Westin because it was labeled family friendly, and it certainly is not. the staff are also not pleasant. 
The pools are not good for little kids or family friendly. for some reason the pool bar is next to the kids slide which is wild. 
The aircon in our room wasn't working, we called maintenance many times and requested to get this resolved and they said nothing wrong with it. in 35 degree heat, this just added to it being an unpleasant stay. 
If you're a family with young ones, I do not recommend this hotel.

reviews.title: Poor service standards
reviews.text:
Please don’t stay at this property. Had my breakfast table given away to a screaming entitled local who wanted a pool view while eating breakfast. Then had my table reset TWICE while I was just going to get food from the morning buffet. 

Ended the stay soaking in rust stained water because returning guest weren’t informed at the lobby to run water clear because of a burst water main. In the end water didn’t turn clear after running the water for almost 45 mins. 
 Ultimately a horrible experience. Left fuming and absolutely wrecked tired, when it was supposed to be a trip to recharge and relax.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe Garden View, Guest room, 1 King, Garden view

reviews.title: Don't stay here!
reviews.text:
The only good thing about this place is its location, which is totally not worth of its price or the ridiculously bad experience with food and cleaniness.
We were offered bad and stinky salami on the pizza we ordered at Prego restaurant inside the hotel and had the terror of almost biting on the giant dead fly that was baked into the sandwich bread for our breakfast box takeout. 
Absolutely unacceptable for a supposed 5-star hotel at US$230 per night.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe Garden View, Guest room, 1 King, Garden view

reviews.title: Westin's Bad in Nusa Dua

reviews.title: OLD AND TIRED

The worst part about staying there was wishing we weren't, especially as we stroll by the other neighbouring resorts which looked much better maintained. One of the worst big-name hotels I've stayed at in Bali.

reviews.title: nice property but not what I expected of a five star property.  Room was damp, noise of construction
reviews.positives: The staff as people were very pleasant, but their ability to work at any speed was beyond slow.
reviews.negatives: The smell of damp in our room was absolutely horrendous. We had to ring nearly everyday to have face towels or mini bar replenished. Every time we went to a restaurant there were so many items on the menu that were not available, we would return to same restaurant 3/4 days later and it was still the same, we went to the Japanese and there were 8 items not available on the menu. As nice as the staff were, they did not know their business and were extremely slow, several times it took up to 20 mins to get a bottle of water. When ordering red wine for our group most of the time they only had 1 additional bottle of the same wine and sometimes not even 1. Really very disappointing.

reviews.title: Disappointment for staying at Westin Resort
reviews.text:
Interior of the room very old .very small for family suite room. The cleaner not clean the floor properly. Still feel dirty. 
The reception front dest are very excellent. They are very helpful especially the concierge desk., they are very good. 
The price of the hotel are too expensive because the room that we get was not worth of the money. 
I wont recomended to anyone .

reviews.title: Before you book please ask if there is any event in the area as you will be severely impacted by it.
reviews.positives: This was the end of our honeymoon. Decided to splurge on a fancy hotel for the final nights and we thought this hotel looks nice. It is indeed a beautiful resort.
reviews.negatives:
It was pretty much the worst hotel stay of our lives. Turns out the world water conference was being hosted in the area of Bali where the resort is located this week and the behavior of the staff because of it was completely unacceptable. 
When we arrived and saw the situation we asked to be moved to a different Marriott Bonvoy property as we realized how bad the restrictions were but they refused to do so.
We were there for five nights and we were not allowed to freely move through the hotel during our stay because of this situation. 
There was massive security presence everywhere, army, navy and police even around the pool and beach which was extremely uncomfortable. 
Resort restaurants were closed up for paying guests if they had any small  group lunch or dinner reservation from groups of people attending the conference. 
They had at times emergency exercises where cars were not even allowed to come in or out of the property. Guests had to walk all the way out of resort grounds, around five or six blocks to have access to a cab. 
My husband got sick after a meal eaten at the resort and I was not allowed access to the in-house drugstore because the hallway it was located on was deemed a “sterile area” because conference guests were going to go through it. I had to take a taxi cab out of the resort  into town to get medicines for him.
Paying over four hundred dollars a night for this kind of service is just so not ok in my book. 
One thing I will say, never again. At least not in the Westin Nusa Dua, Bali.

The staff at the bicycle rental counter didn't inform us that we need to pay for renting the bikes. We were a bit disappinted when they billed us at check out. Also, the taxi the hotel called for airport was terrible. The driver asked us a very high price after we loaded the luggages into the car. Twice the usual rate. He said we brought too many luggages. For some free drinks (infused water) at the pool the adults got told off that it's only for kids although it wasn't indicated and kind of petty. The restaurants at the hotel were way overpriced (12€ for a mediocre chardonnay?) and all the high prices were not even including taxes and service charge (21%). It was kind of disappointing and we won't go back there.

reviews.title: Good. The good compensate the bad experience
reviews.positives:
Staff and their professionalism and very genuine.
Concierge, reception, restaurant and room service and pool bar boy. They were amazing people very friendly and passionate with kids.
Highlight is breakfast team and the menus. Within the peak hours, they served and feed almost 800 people within 4 hours spread. They still remain humble and friendly. One Lady in  Noodle station even remembered that my kids favourite breakfast.
Kids club are amazing. Free bike rental and table tennis were a bonus.
reviews.negatives: Property and rooms was so different than pictures in the booking.com website. I booked pool access room, in my mind, you have a little dip pool which can lead to main pool. That was my understanding. But totally different, pool is about 7m from our rooms. Second the room is super old. I booked 2 rooms and stayed in 1606 and 1608 from 24/12 to 2/1/23. My both rooms has mold on ceiling and wall. 1606 has a creak ceiling with mold and spot mark from condensation. Wifi is super slow.

reviews.title: Average hotel AMAZING SPA
reviews.text:
We were really disappointed with the Hotel Check in and Check out service, you arrive and are left to wander aimlessly to the counter and wait .
We dinned in Prego Italian restaurant, extremely disappointed limited menu , extremely small servings and not enough staff also the Wifi is NOT free. I don't know anywhere that charges for wifi anymore
The only thing saving The Westin is the Heavenly Spa, what an amazing experience! From arrival to completion AMAZING,
If we hadn't experienced this , I don't think there would be any reason to stay at The Westin.

reviews.title: Disappointing Experience
reviews.text: Space constraints - summarized 25-29Dec. Family of 3 (mother, two children 16 and 13 years) were not happy with our stay. Delayed and cumbersome check-in on 25th Dec2019. The outdated Room 2047 very different from the picture on the website - was hastily readied for us, thus not very clean looking. Numerous issues that took hours to solve, very slow service: Cool Bar not cooling; Air-conditioner leaking; room areas not dusted; the worst - one bed mattress reeking of urine with clean sheets on top. Complained to management. Mr Eka (manager) apologized and showed us two other rooms and we were relocated to Room 2211. Definitely cleaner room and better location. Kind Mr Eka seemed to be the only one who was concerned for us, offered an additional bed free of charge. Upon my request, 3rd person breakfast waived, but the hotel really should have offered us the first night for free. Disappointing!! Pros - Private beach; great breakfast buffet, fast checkout. Would I recommend to others? No!

reviews.text: We stayed in Bali, half at Westin Ubud and other at Westin Nusa Dua and there is a world of a difference in the experience we had.  Westin Nusa Dua doesn’t come close to Ubud. reviews.text: the resort is nice... it's huge resort and it's by the beach which is great but there comes some issues with that. You are far away from anything and everything. If you are looking at going to a resort and just stay and enjoy the resort, this is great but if you want to go out and about this location is terrible. The beach was suppose to so nice.... well i was skeptical when reviewing Bali before we left and found little to no pics of tropical beaches...... now i know why. the water is clear but the color isn't pretty. If you want nice beaches go to the Philippines, Thailand of course my favorite Maldives but not Bali. What irritated me the most was checking out.... my visa cards wouldn't work.... 5 star hotel come on now... seriously..... their solution was to use the atm multiple times to take cash out to pay.... I complained about the $5 fee that i get every time i take money out and guy didn't even apologize. left a bad taste in my mouth to an already disappointing trip to Bali. Glad to leave.

reviews.title: 5 stars hotel with 3 stars experience
reviews.text:
This is my first time staying in The Westin Nusa Dua. As one of the best 5 stars hotel in Nusa Dua, I was expecting something more in The Westin services. But all I got was bad experience.

I arrived at The Westin around 8 PM.

First of all was the porter who went into the room together with me as guest. I don't really know about the hospitality standard but in the other hotels that I've ever stayed, the porter always comes separately with the guest. After I arrived in my room, the porter put my luggage in the room eventhough I said he could put it in the door. In this pandemic situation I don't feel comfortable to let people come into my room. After that the porter also tried to show me all the facilities inside the room and walked all over the room eventhough I already said "Makasih Pak saya sudah tau" but he continued and imposed to show me the hairdryer inside the bathroom. What a very excellent service it is.

Sadly it wasn't the ending. The electricity suddenly went out. I thought it was all the room, but when I opened the door it's only my room. I called the hotel express services but I have to wait at least 20-30 minutes until the electricity engineer came. I asked the hotel to get a new room for me and their respond so amazed me. They said sorry and please wait in high tone. Standing applause for their services..

Other than that, the breakfast choices not much.

The only thing that I love about The Westin is their beach. They really have great location. 

All in all, thank you for the services but if I can choose, I won't stay here again eventhough they are on discount.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe Garden View, Guest room, 2 Double

reviews.title: Friendly Staff - Good Facilities
reviews.text:
Good place to stay for Parents with kids. The staff has made it about the kids as much as possible.

4 years and above is the sweet spot to enjoy most of the programs as well as the slide.

The kids club, free ice cream once a day between 12-2pm. Kids spa, kids manicure, pedicure, hair braiding, facial.

Friendly squirrels who are tame enough to feed. Decent breakfast spread, but perhaps 3-4 days in you would get bored, but then there is the option for Prego Brunch upgrade.

Rooms are decent sized. We took the family suite with 2 separate rooms that allowed for some privacy still because they are essentially 2 separate rooms but with an extended common living area.
Bed was too soft for me personally.
reviews.room_type: Family Suite, 2 Bedroom Suite, Bedroom 1: 1 King, Bedroom 2: 2 Double

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
It's time to rejuvenate after decade of working of long hours, i choose to stay in westin for the best price i could get with the direct beach access. Noone has ever mentioned that the hotel was a bit aging, but never mind as i have found the room was okay for the cleanliness. one tiny minus is there's no mini bar in my selected room. Since i support the eco-green environment, i didn't expect the cleaner to change my towels n bedsheet, well i'm not sure on that either but everytime they clean up i found the towel back to where it belongs as the first time. Never mind, just my curiousity. However, in the last day they not coming back with floor mat in the bathroom, guess they're just plainly forget to put it back :D

Btw, let me take a credit for some of their awesome staff: 

- Tika, for helping my husband make a flower arrangement for me and send to the room the next morning after our arrival. That's so sweet of her to help make that happened

- Widi and Yoga, the apprentice & trainee staff for being so attentive to the guests while we were on the beach. I was once experienced different treatment from the Ikan Restaurant Staff as they reject my order to take to the beachfront - which i witness the other guest has same order and they're okay with that! and this two guys was nicely calming me down and apologise for that. I gave them credit because they're not differentiate the guest, whether it's local or overseas guest. 

all in all, i will recommend everyone who like to have a lot of free time with fun activities to stay in westin. wish to come back anytime soon!

My boyfriend and I checked our bags and in excitement ran to the beach to relax. Unfortunately we were asked multiple times by security and staff if we were staying at the Westin in an accusatory manner ( guess they get a lot of beach walk ups trying to use the facilities) which really put a damper on the start of the stay and made me feel very uncomfortable. All staff eyed us as outsiders almost the entire time on the beach. I explained that we were just waiting for our room and had spoke to management prior/ showed bag check ticket/ confirmation papers but still same attitude. The rooms are very nice and the pools are great! Beach itself is a bit of a let down...there's a bit of trash and the beach breaks very intensely...I reccommend jibaran for calmer waters. low tide however is amazingly fun! Overall had a good experience by the end of it but staff should probably warn people that if they come to early for their rooms that they will not be welcome with open arms

reviews.title: Good hardware but bad service and no free wifi
reviews.text:
Actually, all the hardware inside the hotel was very good. However, the transportation service was really extremely bad and it even worse than a 3 stars hotel in Bali.
 
On the first day there, I wanted to take the free transfer from the hotel to "Bali Collection".  The hotel provided a schedule of the free transfer. Then, I decided to take it at 18:05 and had arrived the waiting area before 5 minutes. However, when it was 18:10, the shuttle still did not arrive, the staffs there asked me to keep waiting as sometimes it would be a bit late. I kept waiting till 18:25 and was annoyed because the shuttle still did not come. 

I complained about it to the staffs at that moment, but they said the shuttle service was provided by "Bali Collection" and it was none of their business to control the service, even they could not make any call to the driver for the follow up. It really made me very disappointed, as I never expect it was the service standard there.

Finally, I ask the staffs to call a taxi for me and sometimes when I want to go out, the staffs would help to call taxi. In fact, there are different taxi groups in Bali and I only trust "Blue Bird" which there are many good comment from travel book and the staffs of a 3 star hotel I stayed before Westin. Unfortunately, every time I had requested "Blue Bird" from the staffs, but they every time called another group of taxi without meter, then I always paid higher fare for the taxi service. On the last day in Bali, I was going to go to the airport from the hotel. As the distance is quite long, I wanted to take "Blue Bird" with the most reasonable fare. However, the staffs again told me that if I want "Blue Bird", I have to wait a period of time, but if I ordered another taxi, it would come immediately. At that moment, I was in hurry to the airport, so I had no choice. 

On the other hand, I did not expect that there is no free wifi service provided at all rooms. How do you think about it?
reviews.positives: good hardware, nice rooms

reviews.title: Nice hotel but......
reviews.text: It started off as a lovely vacation until it was marred by the presence of an influx of wedding guests. The hotel staff was overwhelmed by the sheer number of guests who dominated the common areas & left much the areas much to be desired by the other guests. Other hotel guests felt like they were intruded by another party. The wedding went on for three days & the guests were not compensated for the inconvenience caused!

reviews.title: Great for children but tired and needs updating
reviews.text:
I’m in two minds about our stay at the Westin Nusa Dua. The customer service was outstanding from the moment we arrived and they were clearly geared towards welcoming families as my son was given a drink bottle and activity pack upon check in.
The room needed updating. Nothing wrong just looking a bit worn out. The bathroom smelt fishy to me but after a couple of days I either got used to it or it disappeared.  They were also renovating some rooms and sometimes the noise was still going at 9pm.
The pool and the kids club were brilliant! My son adored the water slide and the pool bar was perfectly located next to it! All the staff at the pool were fab as well.
The breakfast was not as good as other 5 star hotels we had been at in Bali. The Ikan restaurant food was really good though. The in room dining options were pretty good and some of the best kids meal options we have seen (balanced and healthy, really appreciate that!)
I think ultimately, in terms of it being polished and more aesthetically up to date, the Westin had a very dated feeling but from a parent point of view, my son had a blast and would love to stay there again. And the customer service was excellent.

reviews.title: Ok-ish hotel, I guess
reviews.text:
I'm having problem  describing this hotel.  Yes its an old hotel,  but that is not the problem.  Its clean and staff are good. Just the standard stuff. What bothers me is the fact that all the electric plugs are all UK,  and Indonesia does not UK style plugs. And apparently they have been using this UK plug since the day the hotel was first build, decades ago, and the did not bother to update it. Even funnier, they have this microwave oven in our unit, but not connected to the power, as it uses round plug while the socket is UK plug. Go figure.  Then there is the swimming pool.  Its 1.8m deep. Its too deep for casual play with family and kids, and yet its shape is not suitable for serius swimming either. So I'm scratching my head as who are the target audience for their seimming pool. Even the small private pool inside our room is 1.6m deep. 
Next, TV channels. Plenty enough but signal is bad, mpst channels are grainy. 
Then finally, the beach. Its ok, I guess. Clean and clear. Maybe we came at the wrong time of the year,  as the beach is completely lifeless,  no waves to play.  Not even a small one.

reviews.title: Enjoyable but with some issues
reviews.positives: The staff were always friendly and accommodating. I especially enjoyed the breakfast, which offered a wide variety of options. The facility overall had everything needed for a successful vacation.
reviews.negatives:
What I didn’t like, however, was that it was often noisy. On one side, there was construction work taking place, for example, at the pool, which made it temporarily impossible to relax there. Additionally, even with the room door closed, you could hear every noise in the hallway.

What bothered me the most, though (which is not the hotel’s fault), was that every trip to the beach involved a sales pitch. There are people standing there all day trying to sell you something. In this context, I also found it borderline inappropriate that a staff member at the pool tried to promote a family-run sports service to me. While I don’t want to assume any bad intentions on their part, I still didn’t find it appropriate.

reviews.title: Generally positive except…
reviews.text:
This is an old resort undergoing renovations  upgrade. Facilities are standard as other hotels along Nusa Dua beach. Staff is well trained in service and friendly. Housekeeping is good. Beds are not comfortable ( too soft and gave us backaches).

One negative highlight is the front line foreign exchange services. They are extremely strict and petty on currency notes with smudges, markings, folds, etc. We were rejected twice during exchanging of AUD/USD notes. There  are no displayed guidelines given to guests on what kind of notes are acceptable. We exchanged a nearly mint 100 USD note and the staff manager rejected us because they noticed some unnoticeable smudge on Franklin’s face? Hello? If you require mint condition notes, please state clearly on a notice displayed at the FX counter. Anyway, we exchanged it outside with better rates and no issues at all. 
Ridiculous and causing great inconvenience to us.

reviews.title: HOTEL ROOM / STAFF
reviews.text:
The hotel itself is very good and the staff are friendly (not all but most of them are).  However, the room we stayed at (delux garden view type) is not big enough and the balcony is useless as not even a chair can be put outside.

During my first day in Bali, we came across a dishonest taxi driver who misled us to pay more.  Luckily, when we returned to the hotel and told the transportation counter, the staff there found out who the driver was and asked him to return the money to us.  We are very impressed of their helpfulness and friendliness.  Overall, our experience in Westin Resort Nusa Dua was good and memorable - only the food in the outlets are a bit on the high side especially with the 21% tax.  Perhaps the hotel should give more discount to their guests in spending there.

reviews.title: great bali hotel
reviews.text:
My partner and I stayed in bali for 3 nights, four days. We decided to have one night at westin in nusa dua then two nights at kuta as from our research we found out that nusa dua is very quiet and away from the highly developed kuta area. We were so happy that we chose westin. Hotel is quite dated from the outside as well as in the lobby area but rooms look newly renovated and modern with a balinese touch. The bed is so comfortable, the bathroom is huge. I really liked the tub, as it is in one corner of the bathroom that is enclosed in glass, hence it has natural lighting. Staff is very friendly. Pool is not that big but nice. There is also a saltwater pool available and a water slide for kids. Beach front is also nice and clean, but the sand is very coarse. The beach is private and quiet unlike kuta beach which is crowded. There are nice cabanas in the beach area unfortunately there is a charge per day if you decide to use these cabanas. 

If you happen to stay at westin during the weekend, I highly recommend their seafood buffet. Its about US $60 per person and includes lobsters, crabs, tiger shrimps, sea bass, tuna and a lot more. Its good, really fresh and worth it compared to the overpriced seafood offerings at jimbaran bay. I would suggest coming in early for the buffet because when we were there they run out of lobsters during the first hour of the buffet. Buffet breakfast is decent and they have american, indonesian, chinese and halal offerings.

Overall, we really enjoyed our stay at westin. I highly recommend it!! The only drawback is that its in nusa dua and there is really nothing much to do in that area of bali. Taxis are available though, if you choose to venture out, and the hotel will gladly assist you with getting a cab.

reviews.title: Nice clean resort style hotel
reviews.text: This hotel is very clean, well presented with very attentive staff. The food (mainly western menu) is of a high standard. If you are looking for a quiet, resort type place where you,the family and kids can relax by the pool, beach or gym / spa etc  - then I would recommend it.  We had a lovely few days there over Nyepi (Bali Silent Day) and the hotel served its purpose for those days very well.  I hadn't done my research as well as I would have hoped, as I prefer a little more immersion in the local culture and the ability to get local food etc. I was worried about being stuck for 'silent day' but as it turns out, all places that have guests staying apparently look after them and work on this holiday - good to now for next time. This hotel is within a large manicured zone of hotels with very little 'real bali / indonesia' around  - its great if you want to stay in the hotel, beach, spa etc and just relax, but I'd suggest not for you if you like to wander around and eat in local places / see a bit of balinese life.  As I said the food was great, but I'd like to see a few more indonesian menu items in a hotel in indonesia!

reviews.title: I am very happy except the Hotel's conceirge...
reviews.text:
Generally the place is very good to stay in Nusa Dua and it is value for money. Westin is an international brand and their service is top notch. The staff's are all amazing, check-in was quick and everyone know what to do. The waiters and bar man are attentive and will help you as much as they can.The pool is deep and a special place are available for kids. Breakfast is as what you expected from a 5 Star resort, both local and international. Their happy hour drinks are quite extensive and delish!

The Hotel's concierge really made my stay abit a turn-off because when we ordered a taxi, they immediately hire a Bali Taxi without asking our preference. Bali taxi AKA local taxi are known as touters and sometimes refuse to use meter. When we said we wanted BlueBird they loss interest and said not available. This is not true, they jsut push for local taxi rather then what the customer wants.

reviews.title: Good hotel, great pool, beach and breakfast.  Good food.
reviews.text:
A beautiful hotel.  I actually had a bad start to my stay their but the majority of the staff, the restaurants we frequented and the pool and beach (and waterslide for the kids) won us all over during our stay.  I would split my stay in Bali to include outside the Nusa Dua enclosure, but the hotel was a beautiful place to stay.   A danger was that the hotel pool/beach was so good it was hard to get the kids out to sight-see :-D  Great breakfast.
A criticism was that our room (intended to have three people) didn't have a table and chairs but instead an embedded desk for a single person to sit at, room service wheeled in a trolley-table which barely fit with no chairs (so sit on the side of the bed to eat; again three people, two of which were kids on white bedding) and blocked off part of the room, and I had to try and get it back out when finished.  I expected better, but it was a poor start for our first night.  The room was also relatively small and could do with a refresh.

reviews.title: Great for families
reviews.text:
Stayed 4 nights with total 3 adults and 3 children. Booked connecting rooms (2 double beds each) and was given a free upgrade. However, when we entered the room, we were given a room with 2 double beds and another room with a king bed. I would have preferred 4 double beds in 2 rooms though, but anyway we were still ok with it. Beds were very comfortable & bathroom is spacious.

The hotel is within walking distance to the beach, beach is very clean and has daybeds to relax on. Baby pool is sheltered. Breakfast was great with lots of selection. Do not have to worry about lack of food as there are plenty of restaurants. 

There is a shuttle bus to Bali Collection, which is the only place for more restaurants and shopping, but things there are generally quite expensive. But overall this is the hotel to go to if you are looking for a relaxing holiday without moving around much.

reviews.title: So family-friendly
reviews.text:
Stayed here for three nights with a young child and loved it.

This hotel is very kids-friendly although most activities are aimed at older kids (I'd say 4 and above) including fishing, arts, cooking classes, drawing, etc.

The facilities are fantastic. the hotel has his own private beach, several pools including kids pool and a water slide. It offers babysitting services for parents wanting to enjoy some quiet meal in one of the restaurants. 

The rooms are spacious with safety deposit boxes, large bathrooms (with tub), comfy beds with choice of pillows and a small balcony. 

Staff is extremely friendly and helpful. The concierge Mr. Agung Yatmika took good care of us and helped us booking several activities in and around Bali. The only thing I was missing was an introduction of the property upon check-in since it is such a large property and we didn't know where the breakfast was located the next morning (cause no one had told us). Otherwise a great experience. 

We tried all restaurants (except for the Spanish tapas bar) and they were good. Best one was Prego for Sunday Brunch with many kids activities and great food selection. The beachside restaurant was also great for some local dishes. Japanese restaurant was a bit average. 

Breakfast buffet is excellent with a large selection of international and Asian choices.

The hotel also has a great spa with various massages including for pregnant women. 

Would definitely stay here again.

reviews.title: Good Stay with Some Room for Improvement
reviews.text:
Our stay at the Westin Nusa Dua had plenty of positives. The staff were genuinely lovely throughout and deserve special mention. They were always polite and friendly, but more than that, they seemed genuinely happy to help with anything we needed. They honoured every request we made with a smile, which made a real difference to our experience. The free bicycle and kayak hire was excellent fun and a real highlight of our trip. The breakfast is excellent with plenty of variety across the board, and the food quality throughout the resort was very good. There’s plenty of choice for dining and drinks. The kids club was brilliant for our toddler, with activities running all day, and the pools were pleasant.

There were a few hiccups, though. Despite emailing three times before arrival to request a good room for our family with a two-year-old, we were initially placed opposite ongoing renovations. The staff quickly moved us to a larger, quieter ‘premium’ room after we mentioned it, which was appreciated.

The property is showing its age in places, with some maintenance issues like dirty carpets, broken plaster, and tiles needing attention. They’re clearly working on refurbishments, but a few things still need fixing to fully justify the price point.

Overall, the excellent staff, family facilities, and free activities made for an enjoyable stay. With a bit more attention to maintenance, this would be a fantastic resort.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
reviews.room_type: Deluxe Garden View, Guest room, 1 King, Garden view

reviews.title: not up to the value we paid.
reviews.text:
I am currently staying at The Westin Nusa Dua.
The hotel is beyond amazing — the staff are extremely friendly and helpful, and the breakfast is, without a doubt, one of the best I’ve ever had.
The beach is very clean, the sand is soft, and the swimming pool is fantastic.

So why only 3 stars?

Unfortunately, it’s because of the room. When I checked in, I noticed the floor hadn’t been properly mopped, and the trash can in the bathroom was still full. For context, I checked in around 4 PM, and when I entered the room, there were 4 or 5 staff members rushing to get it ready. I kindly asked them to reclean the room while I went out for lunch.

When I returned, I tried to use the TV, only to find it wasn’t working — you could hear the sound, but the screen was blank. I decided to ignore it, as I didn’t want to wait around for a fix.

The toilet in the bathroom was also quite inconvenient to use.
Worse still, the shower had a ceiling leak, causing cold water to drip on me while I was trying to enjoy a hot shower — very unpleasant.

We also requested a baby cot. It arrived without a pillow or blanket, and when I asked for them, they brought an adult-sized blanket and pillow — which obviously didn’t fit the cot.

At this rate, I was expecting a much more premium level of service and room quality.

The view is stunning, the resort and beach are just wonderful
Its sad the pool closes at 7 pm.
The bathroom tiles and walls have a lot of cracks, chipped paint, definitely showed signs of aging.
Would recommend for a family trip!
but the service is excellent, the pathways are accessible

reviews.title: BEWARE OF MINI BAR CHARGES!
reviews.text:
Hotel has great staffs attending to every need. Although room is a lil bit dated, it's still clean. It's rather small to my liking. Facilities are the best,makes us wanna stay in hotel area.

However beware of the mini bar as they are all charged. I do feel cheated because i asked their staff and they said it's free but they still charged upon checkout. No prices mentioned there either.

reviews.title: Old but gold
reviews.text:
I was at first slightly disappointed by the room, it was older but after day 1, I was sold. It was super clean, the shower was amazing. The hot water for thr bathtub took only a few mins to fill up for a nice soak.

Thr breakfast table was great, the private beach was not crowded, had many sunchairs to share among guests. The pool was simply amazing, I spent 7hrs there, dipping and gg back to my chair to chill, read and lounge around.

The staff goes the extra mile to make everyone happy. Definitely changed my mind abt nusa dua.

reviews.title: Great location, great staff
reviews.text: The Westin resort is fantastic, great location on the beach and I love the fact that all the hotels are connected through a walking path on the beach side. It’s great if you want to go to other hotel restaurants and the staff are friendly and helpful. The rooms are well maintained. Breakfast buffet selection is great but Room service or in-house dining was ok as and also a bit over priced ! Many pools to choose from and also very peaceful and quiet

reviews.title: Always a Good Stay
reviews.text: We have stayed at The Westin Resort Nusa Dua on several occasions and if you book early enough, the value can't be beat. Pros: service, breakfast buffet, comfortable rooms and bed, the incredible beach right on the property, huge pool plus children slide and baby pool, happy hour specials for adults and kids. Cons: needs more attentive service at the beach and pool areas, rooms are starting to get a little run down but still very nice and comfortable plus they have a new refurbished wing. The restaurants are quite overpriced, but you can also head to restaurants in the area. Overall, this is a wonderful place to to stay, especially if you have children as the beach is so calm and safe.

reviews.title: A very clean and beautiful resort
reviews.text:
Everything was great. Just could do with a bigger and more modern pool bar.
Thanks for looking after us well.

reviews.title: Excellent!! Value for Money
reviews.text:
Was provided free upgrade to pool terrace room as they told some leakage issue in my original booked room.. we just loved the upgrade... Was also provided free extra bedding during our stay... Mrs Ayu was excellent and followed up closely with us to ensure if everything was Ok. Kids just loved the breakfast... 

Disappointed with Spa as was undergoing renovation and was available during the day... The staff allowed me to switch my booking to other Marriot Courtyard Spa so I can use my vouchers

Overall, very happy with the stay and will visit again soon

reviews.title: Westin Resort Nusa Dua  on December 2016
reviews.text: A nice hotel which has a jogging track and a nice swimming pool.

reviews.title: Nusa Dua is very relaxing
reviews.text: The beach itself is very nice and if you add the comfort of the hotel facilities, it makes it even more perfect.

reviews.title: Affordable luxury at its best
reviews.text: They have the best beach , flagged left and right with other resorts that guests can access on ground or beach shore. Once you explore, you will realise Westin has the best beach 👍🏼

reviews.title: Highly recommended
reviews.text: Hotel staffs are very friendly and helpful. Once we arrived, we were immediately greeting by the hotel staffs. The food at the hotel is good too. This hotel is ideal for kids as the have an excellent kids club. We love our stay in this hotel and would definitely stay there again next time

reviews.title: Amazing stay
reviews.text: Great experience from beginning to end. We were warmly welcomed at arrival despite the late hour. Welcome kit for kids was a nice bonus. We got upgraded to a terrace room since we were traveling with young kids. Room was a bit small but was comfortable. Beach was beautiful and the day beds were wonderful! Food was good specially at the Indonesian restaurant as well as the snacks by the beach. Service was very good - staff was kind and warm and most were happy to go the extra mile. We plan to come back again soon and bring the rest of our family.

reviews.title: Great Hotel
reviews.text: Great hotel, clean rooms, nice big pool with slide that the kids enjoyed. Friendly staff... Excellent food for both morning buffet and night buffet. Free flow of grilled lobster on a buffet shows class. One negative,Our room air-conditioning was not cool at all and had trouble sleeping at night.

reviews.title: A memorable stay at The Westin
reviews.text:
We spent 2 nights 3 days at the westin for our fam vacation and sure was, we had a blast during our stay there. we tried the Hama be Resto (superb! especially the beef tenderloin robatayaki), and also lunch by the beach (delicious spaghetti carbonara and angus beef burger) and my lil boy got a free hotdog from the kitchen :) .... 
We spent most of our time lingering by the beach underneath the Dream Bed ... it surely a moment that we want to relish it again someday.
next time we make sure to try your Italian Prego resto.. too bad, at this trip we only went there for dessert .. thank u for making our fam vacation as a memorable one...

reviews.title: We will be back! Most comfortable room
reviews.text: Unfortunately we only got to stay in Westin for 3 hours after we booked and paid in full. Our previous cancelled flight had rebooked us right after we check-in. We were so disappointed to leave as the premium room we've got was so so fantastic and the bed and pillow was heavenly comfortable! The room is well design and reasonable sized, very good value to money. We will be back!
reviews.positives: bed, room, beach

reviews.title: Very enjoyable stay
reviews.text: We booked two adjoining rooms and were pleasantly surprised to be upgraded to a family suite that has a common area for the girls. They love the kids club Programme and the swimming pool. We tried the spa Programme and were very satisfied by the treatment and service. Overall a very pleasant stay and looking forward to the next visit!
reviews.positives: Excellent kids club Programme, exceptional spa treatment Programme and great service from staff

reviews.title: Perfect Stay - relaxing and recommended
reviews.text:
Our stay was great! We were lucky enough to be upgraded to a family suite. It's a spacious suite comprising of two separate bedrooms with separate bathrooms and a common living room. The sound proofing of the bedrooms were really good. Cleaniess was outstanding. I can honestly say the cleanest hotel we have ever stayed worldwide! Nice selection of toiletries too and love the little touches of a step stool for kids to reach the sink, two floor mats and given a giveaway cloth bag which is useful for beach toys and shopping.

We were impressed w the kids club as it had 3 different areas for babies/toddlers, young kids and Tweens. The staff were amazing and you could see they were a very caring and genuine group of people who love children. There were fun hourly activities for the kids.

There were several pools joined to one another in the main area. Different depths and also a slide for kids. A staff member was on duty to help oversee that no accidents whilst the slide was being used.

The buffet breakfast had something for everyone from American to continental, Japanese to Indonesian, Chinese to Indian. A good selection of juices and smoothies on offer too. Buffet breakfast was offered at both the beach restaurant and also the Italian Prego restaurant. We really liked the outdoor table with water feature and spectacular garden views.

We had an exceptional dinner at Prego restaurant on one evening. Really good service and great quality food. Loved the homemade bread too. The Ossobucco was super tender, melts in your mouth as it should and the steak was cooked perfectly. 

The only downside to this place is that it's not quite central. However a ten minute walk would get you to the main gate with little shops and also the Bali Collection with more upmarket options. The hotel makes up for this with a free hourly shuttle bus to both locations. 

You really can't go wrong at Westin. Staff are well trained and speak good English and also Japanese.
reviews.positives: Outstanding cleanliness, amazing staff at kids club and great service and food at Prego restaurant

reviews.title: Worth Trying
reviews.text: Love the placement of everything in the room and bathroom. The hotel and room feel comfortable with Balinese feel. Location is good added with free shuttle to several public areas. However breakfast dining area had a lot of flies! Also, slide on the pool are only allowed for children (I called the hotel beforehand and the receiver said the slide can be used for adults).
reviews.positives: Location, rooms

reviews.title: Wonderful Weekend getaway

reviews.title: Resort for A Family Vacation
reviews.text: I came to Bali for honeymoon in 2006 and stayed in Ritz-Carlton. But my stay with Westin was extremely pleasant and relaxing while enjoying many benefits of being a SPG member. My package didn't include kid's breakfast. Hotel recommended a Kid Pass (US$15 per day) for my 7-year-old son to enjoy free meals in hotel. Also there are three swimming pools in hotel. We had much fun there. This is a hotel that I would recommend to my friends and relatives.
reviews.positives: Guest Room, Swimming Pools, and Gardens

reviews.title: will definitely come back
reviews.positives: staff hospitality, helpful, ability to fulfill guest'sersona

reviews.title: Enjoyable vacation at Westin Nusa Dua
reviews.text: We had a party of 9 staying at the Westin. It was a good experience overall. Staff were friendly and attentive. Facility was well maintained and clean. Room size was a nice size and fairly well layout. Ample towels and pillows. One small negative was the poolside service was slow and disorganised and then breakfast at the verandah was ok. Our meal at Ikan was great and the female singer and her acoustic guitar accompaniment was food and entertaining.
reviews.positives: Facilities, food at Ikan and the Heavenly Spa

reviews.title: Fantastic Service! Nice rooms :)
reviews.text:
We were celebrating our anniversary so we choose to stay at The Westin after reading all the reviews. Glad that choose it as we totally enjoy our stay here. 
- Sunday Brunch at Prego is a Must!!! A superb selection of food and fantastic attention From the service staff. A wide range of things for the kids to do as well.
- we have seen enough good things about the kids club to begin to plan our next trip there with the kids.
- the front counter staff remember is and greeted us by name when we bump into them while moving around in the resort.
- the only part which is not the highlight might be the flies and mosquitoes which I think is not within their control.
Overall, we absolutely love our stay there!
Kind Regards,
Mr and Mrs Ng
reviews.positives: Rooms, pool and service staff
